persona.name: Priya Sharma

occupation.title: Pharmacy Technician
occupation.description: Fills prescriptions, manages inventory, and counsels customers on OTC products. Her pharmaceutical knowledge spills over into how she evaluates skincare — she reads ingredient lists the way she reads drug labels. Works rotating shifts including some weekends.

persona.education: Associate's degree in Pharmacy Technology from College of DuPage, 2023. Currently taking pre-requisite courses online to eventually apply to pharmacy school. Strong science background.
long_term_goals: Get accepted to pharmacy school (UIC is her top choice)
Clear her hyperpigmentation completely and help others with similar concerns
Save enough for a down payment on a condo
Start a skincare review blog focused on ingredient analysis for melanin-rich skin
persona.style: Priya is measured and precise in conversation — she speaks like someone who double-checks facts before stating them. She dresses modestly and practically, favoring earth tones and comfortable shoes for long shifts. Not flashy but always neat. She'll pull up a PubMed study on her phone mid-conversation to prove a point about niacinamide concentrations. Quiet confidence with a dry, understated sense of humor.

traits: Analytically minded — needs evidence before trusting claims
Patient and methodical in her approach to everything
Introverted but opens up passionately about topics she knows well
Can come across as overly cautious or dismissive of trendy products
Self-disciplined about saving money and sticking to routines
Quietly ambitious — doesn't broadcast her goals

interests: Skincare ingredient research and clinical studies
Cooking South Indian food — her mom's recipes
True crime podcasts during long commutes
Photography — mostly food and product flat-lays
likes: Products with published clinical efficacy data
Drugstore products that perform as well as prestige brands
The COSRX and The Ordinary product lines
Comparing ingredient lists side by side
Organized spreadsheets tracking her product rotation
When customers at Walgreens ask her for skincare advice
dislikes: Brands that use proprietary blends to hide ingredient concentrations
Influencers who recommend products without understanding the ingredients
The markup on prestige skincare with the same actives as drugstore versions
People who dismiss hyperpigmentation as 'just dark spots'
Being pressured to buy something on the spot
Products marketed as 'brightening' that are actually just exfoliants

beliefs: Science should guide skincare decisions, not marketing
Affordable skincare can be just as effective as luxury products
Melanin-rich skin has specific needs that mainstream brands often ignore
Patience and consistency matter more than product-hopping
skills: Reading and interpreting pharmaceutical and cosmetic ingredient lists
Budget optimization — finding the best value per active ingredient
Building customized routines based on skin type and concerns
Statistical thinking from her science coursework

general: Researches any new product for at least a week before purchasing
Keeps a detailed skincare journal tracking reactions and progress
Photographs her skin monthly under the same lighting conditions
Will talk someone out of an expensive product if a cheaper alternative exists

morning: Gentle cleanser, niacinamide serum, moisturizer, SPF 50 (PA++++)
South Indian filter coffee — makes it herself
Packs lunch for her shift to save money
Quick check of Reddit skincare subs during her commute
workday: Fills prescriptions and manages pharmacy inventory
Mentally notes when customers ask about skincare OTC products
Lunch in the break room with her meal prep
Studies for pharmacy school prereqs between customers
evening: Alternating actives: tranexamic acid serum Mon/Wed/Fri, azelaic acid Tue/Thu
Sheet mask while watching a true crime documentary
weekend: Browses Ulta or H Mart for new K-beauty finds
Calls her parents in the suburbs
Works on her skincare ingredient comparison spreadsheet

health.physical: Healthy but deals with post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation (PIH) on her cheeks and forehead that she's been treating for two years. Mild iron deficiency she manages with supplements. Standing all day at work causes occasional back pain.
health.mental: Generally stable and grounded. Occasionally feels frustrated by the slow progress on her hyperpigmentation. Finds her research and tracking habits calming rather than obsessive.

relationships.name: Anjali Sharma
relationships.description: Traditional South Indian homemaker who uses turmeric and gram flour for skincare. Priya respects her mother's knowledge but wishes she'd also use sunscreen.

relationships.name: Vikram Sharma
relationships.description: IT consultant. Supportive of her pharmacy school ambitions. Doesn't understand why she has so many bottles in the bathroom.

relationships.name: Fatima Al-Rashidi
relationships.description: Fellow pharmacy tech at Walgreens. They share skincare finds and study for certification exams together. Fatima introduced her to azelaic acid.

relationships.name: Rohit Patel
relationships.description: Dated for a year in college. He now asks her for skincare advice, which she finds amusing. They occasionally grab coffee.
